data.title: AWL Agri shares fall as 6.6% stake sold in block deals in Adani group co, likely for about Rs 2,400 crore
data.description:
AWL Agri Business Ltd saw heavy block-deal activity on Friday, with about 6.6 percent of its equity changing hands in large trades. The stock fell, trading down 2.53 percent at Rs 269.75 in the morning session. The deal could be worth about Rs 2,300-2,400 crore, going by AWL Agri market capitalisation, and an earlier news report.

Adani Group said that the 7 percent block deal in AWL Agri attracted strong institutional demand, with major global and domestic investors participating. According to CNBC-TV18, the group said that Vanguard, Charles Schwab, ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund and SBI Mutual Fund were among the buyers. Tata Mutual Fund, Quant Mutual Fund and Bandhan Mutual Fund also took part in the transaction.

Earlier, CNBC-TV18 had reported citing unidentified sources that Adani Commodities LLP was likely to sell up to 7 percent stake in AWL Agri via block deals. The offer size was pegged at Rs 2,501 crore, with a floor price of Rs 275 per share, representing a 0.63 percent discount to the prevailing market price at the time.

Further details on final allocations are awaited. Adani Wilmar shares are now down 18 percent year-to-date.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Silver Falls on Fed Rate Hold Bets
data.description:
Silver slipped below $50 per ounce on Friday, falling for a second consecutive session amid expectations that the US Federal Reserve will refrain from cutting interest rates in December as policymakers navigate economic uncertainties.

The delayed US nonfarm payrolls report released on Thursday showed mixed signals, with employment growth accelerating in September, but the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%, the highest in four years.

This report will serve as the final labor market data ahead of next month’s FOMC meeting, as the BLS said it will skip the regular October employment release and fold those figures into the delayed November report.

Additionally, Fed Governor Michael Barr cautioned that the central bank must proceed carefully on further rate cuts, with inflation still running above target.

The white metal is on track to lose over 1% for the week.

data.title: Fitch Affirms Quzhou State-owned Capital Operation at 'BBB'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Hong Kong/Shanghai-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Quzhou State-owned Capital Operation Co., Ltd.'s (QZSC) Long-Term Foreign- and Local -Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) at 'BBB'. The Outlook is Stable.

The rating affirmation and Stable Outlook reflect QZSC's continued role as a core government-related entity (GRE) of the Quzhou municipality, as it leads public-policy activities in the region despite the spin-off of its subsidiary, Quzhou Holding Group Co., Ltd.

The ratings are derived from our assessment of the Quzhou government's responsibility and incentive to support factors under our Government-Related Entities Rating Criteria. We look through QZSC's immediate shareholder, which has limited operations and financing role, to the ultimate government sponsor, as QZSC has a policy role and is directly controlled by the sponsor.

The ratings also consider the company's 'b' Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) that is assessed under our Public Policy Revenue-Supported Entities Rating Criteria.

KEY RATING DRIVERS Support Score Assessment:'Virtually certain'

We believe extraordinary support from the Quzhou municipal government to QZSC would be virtually certain if needed, reflecting a support score of 45 out of a maximum 60 under Fitch's GRE criteria. This reflects a combination of responsibility and incentive to support factor assessments.

Responsibility to Support Decision Making and Oversight:'Very Strong'

QZSC is ultimately 98.2% owned and effectively controlled by Quzhou State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission (SASAC), which retains direct decision-making and oversight over QZSC's board member appointments, finances, operations and major corporate events. The government continues to impose specific requirements on QZSC's operations, which need to be closely aligned with the government's strategy, including its key treasury function of centrally managing major local GREs' cash pool.

Precedents of Support:'Strong'

QZSC has continued to receive recurring and sizeable support from the government in the form of subsidies, government repayments, equity injections, special fund allocations, special-purpose bond proceeds, ultra-long treasury bond proceeds and debt swaps. Total government cash payments were around CNY10 billion in 2024, supporting the company's execution of its public mandates and mitigating the financial impact of the spin-off of its subsidiary, Quzhou Holding.

The Quzhou SASAC took over QZSC's 100% stake in Quzhou Holding, as announced in early 2025. The reorganisation is unlikely to dilute government support for QZSC, as Quzhou's major policy-driven GREs remain under QZSC.

Incentives to Support Preservation of Government Policy Role:'Strong'

QZSC preserves the government's policy role by supporting key economic activities and social stability, managing most of the public services in Quzhou, including urban and transport infrastructure construction, social housing, utility supply and industrial park development. The company holds a leading position across these segments, including its role as the city's sole water supplier through its subsidiary, Quzhou Water Group Co., Ltd.

Contagion Risk:'Very Strong'

QZSC remains the reference issuer in Quzhou because of its capital market visibility as the largest bond issuer in the region. QZSC, at the parent level, had the most onshore bonds outstanding, at CNY16 billion in November 2025, most of the proceeds of which was used to repay subsidiaries' debt and fund their operations. QZSC's sizeable consolidated total debt of CNY99.5 billion is mostly for financing public-policy or quasi-policy activities. The concentration poses higher contagion risk to the government than other GREs in Quzhou.

We may reassess the factor if QZSC's peers materially expand their visibility in the capital market or material spin-offs dilute QZSC's position as the region's core government entity, although this is not our base case.

Standalone Credit Profile

The 'b' SCP is based on the combination of a 'b' financial profile, a 'Midrange' risk profile and peer comparison. The notch-specific SCP is at 'b' because QZSC's forecast net leverage ratio of 39.8x by 2029 is comparable with the average of other 'b' rated peers. Nevertheless, the high leverage is mitigated by the company's ample liquidity and access to funding, as well as our anticipation of continued strong government support.

Risk Profile::'Midrange'

Our 'Midrange' risk profile assessment reflects 'Midrange' revenue, expenditure, and liability and liquidity risks.

Revenue Risk::'Midrange'

QZSC's public-policy and quasi-policy activities across multiple sectors support Quzhou's urban development needs. As a result, its revenue is closely tied to the city's growth prospects. We expect demand in Quzhou to be resilient over the next three to five years, underpinned by economic growth outpacing the national average, supported by Zhejiang's common prosperity initiatives and an upgraded transportation network.

Expenditure Risk::'Midrange'

QZSC's business has well-identified cost drivers with moderate volatility, reflected in its stable EBITDA margin. Operating costs are mainly cost of goods sold, with manageable selling, general and administrative expenses. The key supplies of its business are considered adequate, benefitting from government networks to facilitate its policy mandate as it is the city's main urban developer. QZSC has some flexibility on the timing for expansionary capex on its self-operated, self-sufficient projects. In addition, its major investment and funding plans have to follow municipal government guidelines and be supervised and approved by the government.

Liabilities and Liquidity Risk::'Midrange'

QZSC's debt grew rapidly from a low base, but growth slowed in 2024 amid tight funding conditions for policy-driven GREs. Adjusted debt rose 20% to CNY107.8 billion by end-2024 after a 45% increase in 2023. QZSC has a medium-term debt repayment profile, with a six-year weighted-average maturity at end-2024, supported by long-tenor loans from state-owned banks for policy and quasi-policy projects.

Liquidity is ample with CNY34.6 billion in debt due in the next 12 months as of end-2024, which can be covered by its CNY10.8 billion in unrestricted cash and CNY39 billion in undrawn uncommitted bank facilities. Funding access remains sound. Total debt comprised 39% bonds, 49% bank loans, and 12% non-standard financing. It maintains solid relationships with big state-owned, joint-stock and regional banks. QZSC remains restricted from new bond issuance under the current policy measures, but it can refinance all its outstanding bonds.

Financial Profile:'b'

The financial profile is derived primarily from the primary metric. QZSC's adjusted net debt/EBITDA has been increasing and we expect it to rise further to 39.8x by end-2029, from 33.9x in 2024, due to large debt-funded investments. We believe the company's liquidity coverage ratio will cushion its weak debt servicing and interest coverage metrics.

Peer Analysis

QZSC's closest peer is Yichun Development Investment Group Co., Ltd. (YDIC, BBB/Stable). Both are core government entities in their respective regions, play leading roles in the public sector, and consolidate the majority of local state-owned assets. Both are under the effective control of their government sponsors and have historically benefitted from strong financial support. They are also well recognised in capital markets as the primary local GREs supporting urban development and broader economic growth.

Issuer Profile

QZSC is 98.2% ultimately owned by Quzhou SASAC through Quzhou State-owned Assets Holding Group Co., Ltd., and 1.8% by the Zhejiang Finance Bureau through Zhejiang Finance Development Co., Ltd.

QZSC is the leading platform in Quzhou that consolidates major GREs. It is mainly responsible for urban and transport infrastructure construction and investments, social housing projects, functional zone development and utility supply in the city. It also undertakes more commercially driven activities, including hotels and retail management, property development and leasing, and commodity trading.

Key Assumptions

Fitch's rating case is a "through-the-cycle" scenario, which incorporates a combination of revenue, cost and financial risk stresses. It is based on 2020-2024 historical figures and 2025-2029 scenario assumptions:

- Average operating revenue growth of 8.0% a year, driven by Quzhou's urban infrastructure demand growth prospects;

- Average operating expenditure growth of 8.6% a year, reflecting a stable cost structure;

- Net capex (capex minus capital revenue) to average about CNY8.4 billion per year to support the city's economic development needs;

- Net adjusted debt to reach around CNY139 billion based on estimated funding needs, counterbalanced by fiscal support.

Rating Sensitivities Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

-- A lowering of Fitch's credit view of Quzhou municipality's ability to provide subsidies, grants or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations; or

-- A deterioration in the GRE support score to below 45 points.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

An upward revision in Fitch's credit view of Quzhou municipality's ability to provide subsidies, grants or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

References for Substantially Material Source Cited as Key Driver Rating The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria.

Quzhou State-owned Capital Operation Co., Ltd.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; BBB; Rating Outlook Stable

----; Local Currency Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; BBB; Rating Outlook Stable

data.title: Oil extends decline by 1.5% on hopes for Russia-Ukraine peace deal
data.description:
By Helen Clark and Trixie Yap

Oil prices fell 1.5% on Friday, extending declines for a third straight session as the United States pushed for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal that could swell global market supply, while uncertainty over its rate cuts curbed investors' risk appetite.

Brent crude futures fell 93 cents, or 1.5%, to $62.45 a barrel by 0416 GMT, after slipping 0.2% in the previous session. U.S. West Texas Intermediate was down 1.7%, or 98 cents, at $58.02 a barrel, after ending Thursday down 0.5%.

Both contracts are set to fall more than 2.5% this week on oversupply concerns, erasing most of last week's gains.

Market sentiment turned bearish as Washington pushed for a peace plan between Ukraine and Russia to end the three-year war, while sanctions on top Russian oil producers Rosneft and Lukoil are set to take effect on Friday.

"Oil extended declines as Zelenskiy agreed to work on a US- and Russia-drafted peace plan, with U.S. sanctions on two Russian oil majors due Friday," Saxo analysts said in a client note, referring to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy.

"With Ukraine yet to formally reject the deal, the slim odds of an agreement are weighing on prices, as it would remove much of the war's geopolitical risk premium baked into crude," IG market analyst Tony Sycamore said in a note.

However, some analysts were sceptical just how soon a peace deal could be struck.

"An accord is far from certain," ANZ analysts told clients in a note, adding that Kyiv has repeatedly dismissed Russia's demands as unacceptable, hindering any breakthrough.

"The market is also becoming sceptical that the latest restrictions on Russian oil companies Rosneft and Lukoil will be effective."

Lukoil has until December 13 to sell its huge international portfolio.

A stronger dollar was also depressing oil prices as it makes the dollar-denomined commodity more expensive for holders of other currencies.

Dollar strength against Asian currencies was very evident after investors further downplayed odds of a follow-up rate cut at the December FOMC, following the release of minutes from its very divided October meet, UOB analysts said in a client note.

The dollar was on track for its best week in more than a month on Friday as investors wagered the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut rates next month.

data.title: American Healthcare REIT Expects Offering to Close Nov. 24 >AHR
American Healthcare REIT Expects $388.8M in Gross Proceeds From Offering >AHR
Asian Chip Stocks Tumble on Renewed AI Bubble Fears
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun and Kimberley Kao

Asian semiconductor stocks tumbled Friday on renewed fears of an artificial-intelligence bubble, tracking U.S. chip stocks' decline overnight.

Shares in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix--the world's two largest memory-chip makers--slumped as much as 6.0% and 10%, respectively, in morning trading. Both were last down well over 5%, erasing most of their gains from the previous day. The benchmark Kospi was recently down around 3%.

The selloff in South Korean chip makers followed a sharp reversal in U.S. semiconductor stocks, including AI-chip maker Nvidia, which gave up early gains to close sharply lower Thursday. Concerns about an AI-bubble resurfaced after a brief rally driven by Nvidia's robust earnings report. Both Samsung and SK Hynix supply high-bandwidth-memory products to Nvidia.

Some investors worry that AI stocks have become overvalued, especially given companies' increasingly aggressive spending plans. An AI bubble was cited as the top tail risk by 45% of institutional investors in Bank of America's latest monthly survey.

A majority of respondents were concerned that companies are overinvesting--a jump "driven by concerns over the magnitude & financing of the AI capex boom," BofA said.

Technology companies are expected to spend trillions of dollars in the coming years on AI data centers, despite questions about how much revenue AI will actually generate.

The survey found that 53% of investors believe AI stocks are already in a bubble.

"Asia is reacting to a combination of global AI-valuation concerns and region-specific pressures," said Tareck Horchani, head of prime brokerage dealing at Maybank Securities.

"The reversal in Nvidia and the broader U.S. tech space has forced investors to reassess whether AI spending can deliver near-term earnings, which directly hit markets like Korea and Taiwan where semiconductors dominate benchmark weights," Horchaini said.

Heavier foreign outflows are being seen in the Kospi, as reports of memory-chip buyers diversifying toward Chinese DRAM suppliers have amplified concerns that Korean chip makers' current pricing strength may be peaking, he said.

Taiwan's Taiex was last down 3.4%, dragged lower by heavyweights Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing and Foxconn Technology Group, both of which fell more than 4%. TSMC and other major electronics makers declined as investors reassessed global AI demand and questioned the sustainability of data-center spending amid rising inventories across the supply chain, according to Horchaini.

In Japan, a tech selloff was compounded by renewed yen volatility, which has left exporters grappling with softer global demand and currency uncertainty, he said. SoftBank Group and Advantest each plunged over 10%, dragging the Nikkei Stock Average down more than 2%.

In Hong Kong, Chinese chip makers Semiconductor Manufacturing International and Hua Hong Semiconductor each lost more than 5%.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com and Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

Japan Approves $135 Billion Stimulus Shot to Help Households, Economy
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Japan's cabinet has approved $135 billion of stimulus to help households cope with rising living costs and boost economic growth, launching the first fiscal salvo under new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

The Takaichi administration on Friday signed off on the package totalling 21.3 trillion yen, equivalent to $135.27 billion.

At the heart of the package are measures to support households under financial strain, a key issue for Japanese voters. The new government, which took power last month, has pledged to tackle those issues.

The newly approved package included immediate relief measures like energy subsidies and a cash handout of Y20,000 per child, as well as more long-term moves such as raising the income tax threshold and abolishing a provisional gasoline duty.

Measures to spur investment in sectors such as semiconductors and artificial intelligence also featured.

The major fiscal expenditure comes as no surprise given Takaichi's expansionary stance on macroeconomic policy. While the increased spending will likely be supportive of the economy and stock markets, it could add to concerns about the nation's fiscal health. Japan's debt is more than double the size of its economy.

Those worries are reflected in Japanese government bond yields, which have been rising since Takaichi took office in October. On Thursday, the yield on benchmark 10-year JGBs hit 1.835%, the highest level since June 2008.

Takaichi has called for "responsible proactive fiscal policy," but it remains to be seen how her administration will balance more spending with fiscal prudence.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Fitch Maintains TS Financial Holding and Taishin Securities on Rating Watch Negative
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Taipei-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has maintained the 'BBB' Long-Term Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) and 'A+(twn) National Long-Term Ratings on TS Financial Holding Co., Ltd. (TSFHC) and its subsidiary, Taishin Securities Co., Ltd. (TSS), on Rating Watch Negative (RWN).

This follows Fitch's rating action on 14 November 2025, which maintained the Rating Watch Evolving (RWE) on the ratings of Shin Kong Life Insurance Co., Ltd. (SKL, BBB-/A(twn)). SKL became one of TSFHC's main subsidiaries following the completion of the merger between Taishin Financial Holding Co., Ltd. and Shin Kong Financial Holding Co., Ltd. (SKFHC) in July 2025.

A full list of rating actions is below.

Key Rating Drivers

Uncertainties at SKL Remain: The RWN on TSFHC reflects our continued view that uncertainty over SKL's balance sheet strength could have a negative effect on TSFHC's credit profile, given SKL's size and weaker credit profile relative to TSFHC's bank subsidiary, Taishin International Bank Co., Ltd. (TIB, BBB+/AA-(twn)/Stable/bbb+).

The RWE on SKL reflects Fitch's view that valuation of the merged entity's invested assets, when SKL merges with Taishin Life Insurance Co., Ltd (TSL, not rated), which is scheduled for January 2026 will have an uncertain impact on SKL's capital position. There is also a risk of pressure on SKL's capital profile from a potential sharp and sustained appreciation in the Taiwan dollar against the US dollar.

Consolidated Credit Profile: Fitch's assessment of TSFHC considers the standalone creditworthiness of its two main businesses - banking and life insurance. We estimate that the banking and life insurance subsidiaries accounted for around 51% and 46%, respectively, of the group's consolidated assets as of end-September 2025, while securities operations made up around 3%. We adopt a blended approach to assess the group's ratings, given TSFHC has become a financial holding company focusing on both banking and insurance.

Enlarged Scale and Franchise: TSFHC's Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) and Viability Rating (VR) reflect our expectation of benefits from a larger, more diversified franchise after the SKFHC merger, which made the group the fourth-largest financial holding company in Taiwan by total consolidated assets. TSFHC's bank subsidiary will rank sixth by deposits and its life insurance subsidiary will be the third by first-year premiums upon competition of the subsidiary mergers. We expect enhanced cross-selling within the group, which will boost its overall competitiveness.

Double Leverage Remains Significant: Fitch expects TSFHC's holding company leverage, although improving, to remain high. We estimate TSFHC's common equity double leverage ratio (DLR) declined to around 131% by end-September 2025, from 143% at end-June 2025 prior to the merger, as the transaction was funded through the issuance of new common and preferred shares.

TSFHC's regulatory DLR, which classifies preferred shares as equity and its around 2.6% stake in Chang Hwa Bank as other comprehensive income investment, most likely remained moderate at around 116% at end-September 2025, compared to the local regulatory cap of 125%.

No Impact on Short-Term Ratings: We have affirmed TSFHC's Short-Term IDR at 'F3'. This reflects our view that the rating would be affected only by multiple-notch downgrades on the Long-Term IDR, which we do not envisage. The Short-Term IDR is at the baseline option that maps to the Long-Term IDR.

Rating Sensitivities Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

IDRs and VR

Fitch expects to resolve TSFHC's RWN when SKL's RWE is resolved. We will maintain the RWN beyond the typical six-month horizon if the RWE on SKL is prolonged.

TSFHC's VR and Long-Term IDR may be downgraded if we view that SKL's standalone credit profile has deteriorated compared with our previous expectations.

TSFHC's Short-Term IDR would not be downgraded unless its Long-Term IDR is downgraded to 'BB+' or below.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

IDRs and VR

TSFHC's VR and Long-Term IDR may be affirmed with a Stable Outlook if we believe that improvements to SKL's standalone credit profile can be sustained despite market volatility.

We do not anticipate developments that would lead to an upgrade in TSFHC's Short-Term IDR over the near to medium term.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: KEY RATING DRIVERS

National Rating

TSFHC's National Long-Term Rating is at the higher end of the rating scale, reflecting low default risk relative to domestic peers. The RWN is aligned with that on the Long-Term IDR. The National Short-Term Rating is at the baseline option that maps to its National Long-Term Rating.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade:

A downgrade of TSFHC's National Long-Term Rating would arise from a similar rating action on its Long-Term IDR and our view that there is a weakening in its overall credit profile relative to the national rating universe. The National Short-Term Rating would not be downgraded unless the National Long-Term Rating is downgraded to 'A-(twn)' or below.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade:

A stronger credit profile relative to the national rating universe could lead to an upgrade of TSFHC's National Ratings. However, we do not anticipate developments that would lead to an upgrade of its National Ratings in the short term.

SUBSIDIARIES & AFFILIATES: KEY RATING DRIVERS

The rating action on TSS corresponds to that on its parent, TSFHC. TSS's IDRs and National Ratings are solely driven by the company's Shareholder Support Rating (SSR), which is equalised with TSFHC's Long-Term IDR. This reflects our belief that there would be a huge reputational risk to TSFHC if TSS were to default, given the strong linkages between TSS and the group. TSS is fully owned and controlled by TSFHC.

The rating also reflects TSS's small size relative to TSFHC's ability to provide the support required under Taiwan's Financial Holding Company Act, making such support immaterial for the parent.

SUBSIDIARIES AND AFFILIATES: RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Fitch expects to resolve the RWN on TSS when the RWN on TSFHC is resolved. The ratings of TSS will move in tandem with those of TSFHC.

SSR

Any negative rating action on TSFHC's Long-Term IDR would reflect a change in support ability and lead to similar rating action on TSS's SSR. A decline in the group's propensity to support TSS would also lead to negative rating action. This may be indicated by signs of weakening integration, branding linkages and control, or Fitch's perception of a reduced strategic importance of TSS to its parent. However, Fitch believes a change in support propensity is not likely in the near term.

IDRs and National Ratings

Any negative rating action on TSS's SSR would lead to similar rating action on its Long-Term IDR. There could also be a similar impact on the National Long-Term Rating, although any assessment would also incorporate our view on the relativities within the national rating scale.

TSS's Short-Term IDR would not be downgraded unless its Long-Term IDR was downgraded to 'BB+' or below. Similarly, Fitch does not consider downgrading TSS's National Short-Term Rating in the near term unless its National Long-Term Rating falls to 'A-(twn)' or below.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

SSR

There could be positive rating action on TSS's SSR if there was similar rating action on TSFHC's Long-Term IDR.

IDRs and National Ratings

TSS's Long-Term IDR is linked to its SSR. The National Long-Term Rating could also be reassessed if the SSR is upgraded, taking into account the company's support-driven profile compared with other entities on the scale.

TSS's Short-Term IDR and National Short-Term Rating could be upgraded following similar action on TSFHC.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. Public Ratings with Credit Linkage to other ratings

TSFHC's Long-Term IDR is linked to that of TIB and SKL. TSS's ratings are linked to those of TSFHC.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

Taishin Securities Co., Ltd.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; BBB; Rating Watch Negative

; Short Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; F3

; National Long Term Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; A+(twn); Rating Watch Negative

; National Short Term Rating; Affirmed; F1(twn)

; Shareholder Support Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; bbb; Rating Watch Negative

TS Financial Holding Co., Ltd.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; BBB; Rating Watch Negative

; Short Term Issuer Default Rating; Affirmed; F3

; National Long Term Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; A+(twn); Rating Watch Negative

; National Short Term Rating; Affirmed; F1(twn)

; Viability Rating; Rating Watch Maintained; bbb; Rating Watch Negative

data.title: Copper heads for weekly loss on firm dollar as traders assess US job data
data.description:
Copper slipped on Friday and was set for a weekly loss, as a firm U.S. dollar and mixed September jobs data kept investors cautious ahead of the Federal Reserve's December interest rate decision, with China's subdued demand adding further pressure.

The most-active copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange declined 0.82% to 85,670 yuan ($12,045.16)a metric ton as of 0330 GMT, set for a weekly loss of 1.42%.

The benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange dropped 0.39% to $10,696.5 a ton, poised to end the week down 1.41%.

The September job data on Thursday, which was delayed due to the government shutdown, offered mixed signals to the Fed, with policymakers pondering the December rate decision, showing stronger-than-expected growth in new hiring but a rise in the jobless rate to a near four-year high.

The delayed September data will be the last official job data before the December interest rate decision.

Dollar remained strong as many Fed officials were still hawkish, weighing on commodities traded with the greenback by making them more expensive for investors using other currencies.

Meanwhile, demand from China remained weak as prices remained at an elevated level this year.

Shanghai copper surged more than 16% in 2025, while its London peer spiked 22% this year.

The Yangshan copper premium (SMM-CUYP-CN), a gauge of Chinese appetite for imported copper, was at $33 a ton the latest, down from a peak of above $100 in early May this year.

Elsewhere among SHFE base metals, aluminium declined 1.04%, zinc was little changed, nudging 0.04% lower, lead dropped 0.43%, nickel tumbled 1.70% and tin lost 1.27%.

Among other LME metals, aluminium dropped 0.73%, zinc declined 0.86%, lead shed 0.45%, nickel lost 0.59% and tin was down 1.01%.

Friday, November 21

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0700 UK Retail Sales Ex-Fuel MM Oct

data.title: Traders on alert to yen intervention; dollar headed for weekly gain
data.description:
Key points:
  • Yen pinned near 10-month low, down nearly 2% for the week
  • Takaichi's cabinet approves 21.3 trillion yen stimulus package
  • Dollar firms, mixed US jobs report lowers December Fed cut bets

By Rae Wee

The yen languished near a 10-month low on Friday but found some support as Japanese officials stepped up their jawboning to stem the currency's decline, while the dollar was on track for its best week in more than a month.

The yen briefly popped higher in early Asian trading after Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said intervention was a possibility to deal with excessively volatile and speculative moves, leaving traders on alert for signs of yen buying from Tokyo.

The currency was slightly stronger at 157.41 per dollar, though it remained not far from Thursday's 10-month trough of 157.90. It was still on track to lose 1.8% for the week.

Much of the focus in currency markets this week has been on the yen, which has plumbed fresh lows as investors worried about the nation's worsening fiscal position brought about by Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's lavish spending policies.

Takaichi's cabinet approved a 21.3 trillion yen ($135.40 billion) economic stimulus package on Friday, her first major policy initiative.

"The elephant in the room now is mounting intervention risks," said Vishnu Varathan, head of macro research for Asia ex-Japan at Mizuho. "Interventions are likely to be opportunistic and short-lived. Essentially, speed bumps, not barricades."

Tokyo last spent 5.53 trillion yen, or nearly $37 billion, in July 2024 to intervene in the foreign exchange market to haul the yen away from 38-year lows.

Against the euro, the yen was pinned near a record low and last stood at 181.56. Sterling rose 0.07% to 205.94 yen , hovering near a 16-month high.

FED CUT BETS RECEDE

In the broader market, the dollar was set for a weekly gain as investors bet that the U.S. Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut rates next month.

The release of a delayed U.S. nonfarm payrolls report on Thursday painted a mixed picture of the country's labour market, showing employment growth accelerated in September, though the jobless rate rose to 4.4%, its highest level in four years.

That reinforced the view that the Fed is likely to refrain from cutting rates at its December meeting, as policymakers continue to sail through an economic fog brought about by the U.S. government shutdown.

Against the dollar, the euro was pinned near a two-week low and last bought $1.1537, on track for a weekly decline of 0.7%.

Sterling rose 0.14% to $1.3087, though it was set to lose 0.8% for the week, with investors also anxiously awaiting Britain's upcoming budget, a major test for the nation's currency and bond markets.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of peers, flirted with a 5-1/2-month peak and last stood at 100.13. It was on track to record a weekly gain of more than 0.8%, its best performance in over a month.

"The shutdown-delayed September jobs report did not provide clarity on what the FOMC will do at its much-debated December meeting," economists at Wells Fargo said in a note.

"We remain of the view that what the Fed should do is cut the federal funds rate by 25 bps ... That said, what the Fed will do is a separate debate entirely."

They added that their call for lowering rates in December was a "close" one and that a hold "would not surprise us at this point."

Markets are now pricing in just a 27% chance of the Fed easing rates next month. (0#USDIRPR)

Elsewhere, the Australian dollar was up 0.09% at $0.6446 after sliding 0.6% overnight on a broad risk-off mood in markets.

The New Zealand dollar rose 0.11% to $0.5588, having also lost 0.4% on Thursday.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin fell to a seven-month low of $85,387.82, while ether slid more than 2% to a four-month trough of $2,777.39.

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on Nov 21
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 42.4450 against the U.S. dollar at 0410 GMT, after closing at 42.3700 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.70% higher on Thursday at 10,979.73 points.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian shares extended a global rout on Friday as the much-anticipated U.S. jobs data failed to provide clarity on the near-term path for interest rates, with investors returning to dumping risk assets even after Nvidia's earnings dazzled.

ERDOGAN ATTENDS G20 SUMMIT

President Tayyip Erdogan will travel to South Africa for the G20 summit being held this weekend.

PKK PROCESS COMMISSION

The parliamentary commission overseeing issues surrounding the disarmament of the outlawed Kurdistan Workers Party (PKK) militant group hold a meeting to discuss a potential visit by commission member MPs to meet PKK leader Abdullah Ocalan in his island prison. (1100 GMT)

FOREIGN ARRIVALS DATA (TRTARR=ECI)

The Ministry of Culture and Tourism will publish data on foreign arrivals for October. (0800 GMT)

TRADE MINISTER

Minister of Trade Omer Bolat will attend the opening ceremony of Turkey's e-Commerce Week in Istanbul. (0630 GMT)

TRANSPORT MINISTER

Minister of Transport and Infrastructure Abdulkadir Uraloglu will attend various opening and groundbreaking ceremonies in Bursa. (0800 GMT)

PARLIAMENT

Parliament's Planning and Budget Commission will discuss the draft 2026 budget of the Health Ministry. (0700 GMT)

data.title: Ethereum Price Declines Again, Consolidates Below $3K After Latest Breakdown
data.description:
Ethereum price failed to stay above $3,000 and tested $2,770. ETH is now attempting to recover but faces resistance near $2,880.

  • Ethereum started a fresh decline after it failed to stay above $3,000.
  • The price is trading below $3,000 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.
  • There is a key bearish trend line forming with resistance at $3,050 on the hourly chart of ETH/USD (data feed via Kraken).
  • The pair could continue to move down if it settles below the $2,800 zone.

Ethereum Price Dips Further

Ethereum price failed to continue higher above $3,050 and started a fresh decline, like Bitcoin. ETH price dipped below $3,000 and entered a bearish zone.

The decline gathered pace below $2,880 and the price dipped below $2,800. A low was formed at $2,770 and the price is now consolidating losses below the 23.6% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $3,058 swing high to the $2,770 low.

Ethereum price is now trading below $3,000 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average. If there is another recovery wave, the price could face resistance near the $2,920 level and the 50% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $3,058 swing high to the $2,770 low.

The next key resistance is near the $2,950 level. The first major resistance is near the $3,050 level. There is also a key bearish trend line forming with resistance at $3,050 on the hourly chart of ETH/USD. A clear move above the $3,050 resistance might send the price toward the $3,120 resistance. An upside break above the $3,120 region might call for more gains in the coming days. In the stated case, Ether could rise toward the $3,220 resistance zone or even $3,250 in the near term.

Another Drop In ETH?

If Ethereum fails to clear the $2,920 resistance, it could start a fresh decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $2,770 level. The first major support sits near the $2,740 zone.

A clear move below the $2,740 support might push the price toward the $2,680 support. Any more losses might send the price toward the $2,620 region in the near term. The next key support sits at $2,550 and $2,500.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for ETH/USD is gaining momentum in the bearish zone.

Hourly RSI – The RSI for ETH/USD is now below the 50 zone.

Major Support Level – $2,770

Major Resistance Level – $3,050

data.title: SJM slips to 4-1/2-month low amid Hotel L'Arc acquisition, Casino Ponte 16 plans scrapped
data.description:
** Shares of Macau gaming and resort operator SJM Holdings fall 3% to HK$2.63, their lowest level since July 7

** SJM plans to buy Hotel L'Arc, on which Casino L'Arc Macau is located, for HK$1.75 billion ($224.8 million), while it drops plans to buy the property where Casino Ponte 16 is located in Macau

** Casino Ponte 16 will cease operations on November 28 - co

** UBS, with "sell" rating, says L'Arc valuation appears reasonable and the acquisition is viewed as a "net positive" as the deal will be funded by undrawn credit facilities with leverage impact to be largely offset by incremental EBITDA from market share gains at L'Arc

** YTD, stock down 1.9%, while the benchmark Hang Seng Index up 25.7%

($1 = 7.7840 Hong Kong dollars)
